regime_justification: USDJPY back at 161.24 after BOJ hiked to 1.0% on Jun 16; spread still 183 bps but pace slower; MOF verbal warnings continuing, intervention risk elevated despite recent rate hike.

domino_stage.label: Pre-Intervention
domino_stage.justification: USDJPY tested 161.69 high this week, MOF rate-check rumors persist, BOJ just hiked 25bp but yen giving back gains; at watch line with verbal intervention ongoing.

1w.label: Watchful
1w.driver: USDJPY still at 161 handle post-BOJ hike, MOF tolerance thin, vol subdued at ~11%, but Fed hawkish and US-Iran deal capping oil eases immediate pressure.

2w.label: Watchful
2w.driver: Spread at 183 bps keeps carry attractive despite BOJ normalization; MOVE/VIX both calm but setup fragile if USDJPY tests 162 or oil reverses.

3w.label: Unlikely
3w.driver: If US-Iran deal holds and oil stays subdued, import-cost pressure eases; BOJ July meeting unlikely to surprise after Jun hike; modest unwind risk recedes.

4w.label: Watchful
4w.driver: BOJ Aug meeting (7/30-31) looms; market may re-test MOF tolerance or price next hike, keeping tail risk live; scenario-weighted elevated.

bottom_line: Stage 2 (Pre-Intervention): USDJPY at 161.24, just 0.46 yen below 162 watch line. BOJ hiked to 1.0% on Jun 16, dissent from Asada, but pair reversed gains within 48h. US-JP spread 183 bps. MOF verbal warnings persistent (Kihara, Katayama). VIX 16.4, MOVE 67, both calm. WTI $76.51 (-21% monthly) eases ¥/bbl pressure. BTC $63,676 (neutral). Grades: C/C/D/C. Applied unwind prior to 1w (would otherwise grade D given calm vol); 4w elevated on BOJ Aug meeting catalyst. Watch USDJPY break of 162, oil reversal, or Himino/Ueda hawkish follow-through.

trigger_map.note: USDJPY 161.24, just 46 pips from 162; Kihara repeated 'prepared to respond' Jun 19; rate-checks rumored but no action yet

trigger_map.trigger: USDJPY 1M risk reversal flip to JPY-call
trigger_map.note: Vol subdued at 11%, no skew flip yet; would signal hedge demand if USDJPY breaks 162
US-Iran deal signed Jun 20 in Switzerland, Hormuz reopening; WTI down 21% monthly; risk dormant but reversal would reignite
At 67, well below 100; peaked 115 in March; Fed hawkish but rates vol compressed, no regime change yet

narrative.p1: USDJPY closed 161.24 on Jun 19, up 0.10% daily and testing 161.69 intraweek—just 46 pips shy of the 162 MOF watch line. BOJ hiked 25bp to 1.0% on Jun 16 (first since Dec 2025), but the yen reversed all gains within 48 hours as the Fed held hawkish and half the FOMC now expects a hike later this year. Deputy Governor Himino pledged continued tightening on Jun 19, yet dissent from Asada (production/employment drag) and Governor Ueda's hospitalization (liver cyst) add noise. MOF Chief Cabinet Secretary Kihara reiterated 'prepared to respond to excessive moves' verbatim. Oil collapsed 21% this month on the US-Iran preliminary peace deal (signed today Jun 20 in Switzerland), easing Japan's import-cost pressure. Nikkei hit 71,250, absorbing the hike well, but equity breadth is narrow (tech 57% of index). Stage unchanged at 2 (Pre-Intervention); regime remains yellow.
narrative.p2: Computed anchors: US-JP 10Y spread 183 bps (446 - 263), down 1 bp but still wide enough to sustain carry. Carry-to-vol proxy 183/11 ≈ 16.6 (elevated, vol too low for this setup). ¥/bbl crude 12,332 (76.51 × 161.24), down sharply from peaks, reducing BOJ's inflation tail risk. USDJPY 5-day +1.1%, pace moderate but level high. Distance from 60d mean: stretched ~2% above. MOVE 67 and VIX 16.4 both calm, credit spreads tight at 278 bps—cross-asset complacency despite USDJPY at watch line and Fed hawkish pivot. Applied unwind prior to 1w grade: would otherwise be D (vol calm, oil down, BOJ just moved), but USDJPY proximity to 162 and persistent MOF warnings justify C. Grades: C/C/D/C. Week 3 dips to D if US-Iran deal holds and import pressure stays subdued; week 4 back to C on BOJ Jul 30-31 meeting catalyst (unlikely to hike but market may re-test positioning). No FX risk reversals data available; flag noted.
narrative.p3: BTC at $63,676, up 1.6% daily, range-bound 63-66k for two weeks—neutral, no unwind signal. Crypto liquidity stable; BTC decoupled from oil's 21% monthly drop (prior correlation broken). XRP and open interest data unavailable. BTC-DXY 30d correlation not retrieved but DXY near 101, modest strength. Crypto showing resilience despite Fed hawkish tilt and USDJPY stress, suggesting risk-on rotation intact in digital assets even as FX carry setup fragile. For positioning: BTC neutral-to-constructive if USDJPY stabilizes below 162; sharp yen rally (intervention or surprise BOJ move) would likely trigger 5-10% BTC pullback as yen carry unwind hits leveraged positions. Watch BTC support at 62k; break below flags broader risk-off.

horizon_rationale.w1_w2: Week 1 at C: USDJPY 161.24, MOF tolerance thin, but VIX/MOVE calm and oil down 21% buys time. Week 2 holds C: spread still 183 bps keeps carry bid, but if USDJPY tests 162 or oil reverses on deal failure, intervention or vol spike likely. No meaningful catalyst inside 2 weeks to force grade change unless pair breaks 162.
horizon_rationale.w2_w3: Week 2 C → Week 3 D: if US-Iran deal sticks and Hormuz fully reopens, oil stays subdued, ¥/bbl pressure off, BOJ has breathing room. Jul 30-31 BOJ meeting unlikely to hike again so soon after Jun move. Unwind risk moderates in benign oil scenario. Grade drops to D (Unlikely) as carry stabilizes and MOF can tolerate 160-162 range if pace slows.
horizon_rationale.w3_w4: Week 3 D → Week 4 C: BOJ Aug meeting (late July decision for Aug) or market positioning into Sep create scenario risk. If JGB 10Y breaks 2.75% or Himino/Ueda signal Sept hike, USDJPY may re-test highs and MOF patience. Scenario-weighted grade lifts back to C (Watchful) on catalyst uncertainty, not data certainty.

watch_list: USDJPY break above 162.00 (triggers MOF intervention high probability)
Oil reversal above $85 (US-Iran deal failure, Hormuz re-closure, reignites Japan inflation pressure)
BOJ Himino or Ueda speech signaling Sept hike appetite (would compress spread expectations, but also test yen)
MOVE index spike above 90 (rates vol regime change, Fed repricing, carry unwind amplifier)
JGB 10Y break above 2.75% (prior cycle high, confirms BOJ behind curve, accelerates tightening path)
